title: Testing, Release Gates, and Memory Incident Defense · C Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar testing, release gates, and memory incident defense decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for production memory-safety defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine unit, property, integration, fault, stress, sanitizer, fuzz, ABI, resource, load, and restore evidence by risk.
Test supported GCC and Clang versions, optimization modes, architectures, libraries, feature macros, and warning baselines.
Pin source, dependencies, compiler, flags, generated files, symbols, SBOM, provenance, and artifact identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Memory Test Layers · changed case · Quiz Memory Test Layers
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 3559. Produce bounded C evidence to decide release readiness from complementary memory evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
